extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ attacked with approximately 700m remaining on the final climb to Col San Martin, caught solo leader MÄDER inside 20m to go, sealing his third stage win and overall victory.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ deliberately sat on SCHACHMANN's wheel during penultimate acceleration to neutralize him before launching final winning sprint.
BENNETT infiltrated breakaway for training accelerations specifically targeting Milan-San Remo Poggio preparation, abandoned when caught by peloton.
BENOOT launched penultimate attack from chase group forcing ROGLIČ to respond before ROGLIČ's decisive counter-acceleration with 700m remaining.
MÄDER held 18 seconds with 1km remaining but lost on gradient steepening in final 400m when ROGLIČ could deploy sprint power.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: ruthlessly optimized stage hunting after sealing GC, demonstrating climbing sprint power beyond typical GC riders on steep gradients
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Maximilian: neutral: defended second overall but lacked acceleration to counter ROGLIČ's final surge despite attempting to initiate late attacks
rider_signals.MÄDER Gino: positive: held off peloton pursuit for entire climb from breakaway but lacks sprint power on steep gradients against pure climbers
rider_signals.BENOOT Tiesj: positive: second overall 2020, attacked from chase group forcing ROGLIČ response showing retained climbing form despite not contending GC
rider_signals.HAMILTON Lucas: positive: finished fourth isolated from teammates suggesting strong late-race climbing ability under fatigue
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: neutral: used breakaway specifically for Poggio-specific acceleration training intervals, abandoned when peloton caught demonstrating structured preparation
rider_signals.VANHOUCKE Harm: positive: strong mountain performance noted at finish suggesting climbing form building despite no podium placement
